source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/01_DHF/05_HnX-P1, HnX-PB/03_설계개발 출력/10_User manual/[HnX-PB] User Manual(EU) (A-IFU-HNX(B)-EU, Rev 0.5, 260429).pdf

document.title: User Manual (HnX-PB) Portable X-ray System
document.revisionOrVersion: Rev. No. 0.5; Doc No : A-IFU-HNX(B)(EU) Rev No : 0.4

sourceSummary.oneLine: User manual and service information for the HnX-PB battery-powered portable X-ray system.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Provides operating, safety, regulatory, EMC, maintenance, calibration, and product information for use of the HnX-PB.
keyPoints: The HnX-PB is a battery-powered portable X-ray system intended for generating radiographic images of human anatomy.
The device is intended for use by qualified healthcare professionals in professional healthcare facilities and controlled indoor environments.
The device emits ionizing radiation and includes warnings on restricted use, radiation safety, contraindications, and facility access controls.
Regulatory information lists IEC, EN, FCC, FDA guidance, and 21 CFR requirements, including IEC 60601-1-2 EMC compliance.
Service information specifies annual inspection, annual calibration, and up to 7 years estimated product lifetime under appropriate inspection and maintenance.

entities.name: HnX-PB

concepts.name: Intended purpose
concepts.evidence: The HnX-PB is a battery-powered portable X-ray system intended for generating radiographic images of human anatomy.

concepts.name: Indications for use
concepts.evidence: The device is indicated for the diagnostic imaging of the following clinical conditions: Traumatic and Orthopedic Conditions; Respiratory and Thoracic Conditions; Abdominal Conditions.

concepts.name: Contra-indications
concepts.evidence: Pregnancy: The device is contraindicated for use in the abdominal and pelvic regions of women who are pregnant or may be pregnant.

concepts.name: Intended use environment
concepts.evidence: The HnX-PB is intended for use in professional healthcare facilities, such as hospitals, clinics, and emergency departments.

concepts.name: Radiation safety
concepts.evidence: When X-ray is being irradiated, the operator shall be stand at least 1.8meters(6feet) from the patient, the X-ray tube, and out of the useful beam.

concepts.name: Restricted use and access control
concepts.evidence: This device emits ionizing radiation and lacks built-in user authentication. Unauthorized operation may result in inappropriate radiation exposure to patients, operators, or bystanders

concepts.name: Medical equipment classification
concepts.evidence: Type of protection against electrical shock Class I or Internally powered ME equipment

concepts.name: EMC compliance
concepts.evidence: HnX-PB conforms to this IEC60601-1-2:2020 standard for both immunity and emissions.

concepts.name: Bluetooth preview mode
concepts.evidence: A thumbnail image is transmitted from the PC to the Control Unit using Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) wireless communication (module: BoT-nLE521, frequency: 2,402–2,480 MHz), and the Control Unit displays the received image on the LCD screen.

concepts.name: Regular inspection and maintenance
concepts.evidence: be sure to perform regular inspection at least once a year.

concepts.name: Product lifetime
concepts.evidence: The estimated product lifetime is up to 7 years under appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.

evidence.claim: The document is a user manual for the HnX-PB Portable X-ray System.
evidence.evidenceText: User Manual (HnX-PB) Portable X-ray System

evidence.claim: The document header lists revision date 2026.04.29 and revision number 0.5.
evidence.evidenceText: Portable X-ray System Rev. Date Rev. No. Page User Manual 2026.04.29 0.5 1 / 66

evidence.claim: The document number is A-IFU-HNX(B)(EU), with a repeated document header revision number of 0.4.
evidence.evidenceText: Doc No : A-IFU-HNX(B)(EU) Rev No : 0.4

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is a portable X-ray system.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB is a portable X-ray System.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is battery-powered and intended for generating radiographic images of human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB is a battery-powered portable X-ray system intended for generating radiographic images of human anatomy.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB consists of an LCD display, X-ray generator, X-ray tube assembly, and collimator.
evidence.evidenceText: This portable X-ray system (model: HnX-PB) consists of an LCD display with up and down soft-keys for kV control, an X-ray generator (power transformer), an X-ray tube assembly, and a collimator.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is intended for professional healthcare facilities such as hospitals, clinics, and emergency departments.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB is intended for use in professional healthcare facilities, such as hospitals, clinics, and emergency departments.

evidence.claim: The target population is adult patients.
evidence.evidenceText: Target Population • Age: Adult patients.

evidence.claim: The device is not intended for pediatric patients.
evidence.evidenceText: The device is not intended for use in the Pediatric population, which includes Children (patients aged 2 years and above) and Infants (patients under 2 years old).

evidence.claim: The device is contraindicated for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Mammography: This device is not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The device emits ionizing radiation during normal use.
evidence.evidenceText: This device emits ionizing radiation during normal use.

evidence.claim: Operators should stand at least 1.8 meters from the patient and X-ray tube during irradiation.
evidence.evidenceText: When X-ray is being irradiated, the operator shall be stand at least 1.8meters(6feet) from the patient, the X-ray tube, and out of the useful beam.

evidence.claim: The device requires facility-implemented access controls because it lacks built-in user authentication.
evidence.evidenceText: This device emits ionizing radiation and lacks built-in user authentication. Unauthorized operation may result in inappropriate radiation exposure to patients, operators, or bystanders

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is specified as Class I and IPX0 equipment under EN 60601-1 standards.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB is specified as Class I and IPX0 equipment under EN 60601-1 standards.

evidence.claim: Medical equipment classification includes Class I or internally powered ME equipment and IPX0 ingress protection.
evidence.evidenceText: Type of protection against electrical shock Class I or Internally powered ME equipment Degree of protection against ingress of water IPX0

evidence.claim: The device conforms to IEC 60601-1-2:2020 for immunity and emissions.
evidence.evidenceText: HnX-PB conforms to this IEC60601-1-2:2020 standard for both immunity and emissions.

evidence.claim: The product safety standards include IEC 60601-1, IEC 60601-1-2, IEC 60601-1-3, IEC 60601-2-28, IEC 60601-2-54, IEC 62304, IEC 62366-1, IEC 60601-1-6, and EN ISO 15223-1.
The manual lists FDA guidance documents and 21 CFR 1020 requirements as addressed or conformed to.
evidence.evidenceText: All applicable aspects of these guidance documents listed in these 510(k) summaries have been addressed. The device also conforms to the following: 21 CFR 1020 Subchapter J: Performance Standards for Ionizing Radiation Emitting Products 21 CFR 1020.30: Diagnostic x-ray system and their major components

evidence.claim: The maximum output of the X-ray generator is 720 W.
evidence.evidenceText: The maximum output of X-ray generator is 720W

evidence.claim: The X-ray tube output voltage range is 50 to 90 kV.
evidence.evidenceText: The X-ray Tube output voltage of generator is 50~90kV

evidence.claim: The X-ray tube focal spot is 0.5 mm x 0.5 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: The X-ray Tube focal spot is 0.5 ㎜ x 0.5 ㎜

evidence.claim: The firmware is HnX_PB version 00.13.
evidence.evidenceText: Software(Firmware): HnX_PB Version: 00.13

evidence.claim: Bluetooth thumbnail preview uses BLE module BoT-nLE521 at 2,402 to 2,480 MHz.
evidence.evidenceText: A thumbnail image is transmitted from the PC to the Control Unit using Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) wireless communication (module: BoT-nLE521, frequency: 2,402–2,480 MHz)

evidence.claim: Regular inspection should be performed at least once a year.
evidence.evidenceText: In order to ensure the safety of patients and operator, to maintain the performance and reliability of the device, be sure to perform regular inspection at least once a year.

evidence.claim: Annual preventive inspection and maintenance includes checking for oil spills, annual calibration, and collimator light field deviation.
evidence.evidenceText: The following inspection and maintenance for the unit shall be performed once every year, and handled by a service person only: a) Check if the oil tank of the Portable X-ray System has any oil spill from the outer wall of the tank, any liquid surface drop in the tank. b) Calibrate the Portable X-ray System annually. c) Check if the collimator light field deviates from the tube radiation field.

evidence.claim: The estimated product lifetime is up to 7 years under appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.
evidence.evidenceText: The estimated product lifetime is up to 7 years under appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.

evidence.claim: H&abyz Co., Ltd. is listed as the manufacturer and the product is made in Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer: H&abyz Co., Ltd. ... Manufacturing country: Made in Korea

evidence.claim: Javitech Consulting GmbH is listed as the European Authorized Representative.
evidence.evidenceText: European Authorized Representative Javitech Consulting GmbH Sachsenhausener Str. 16, 65824 Schwalbach a. Ts. Germany

reviewFlags.reason: Revision metadata is conflicting: page headers list Rev. No. 0.5 with date 2026.04.29, while repeated document headers state Doc No : A-IFU-HNX(B)(EU) Rev No : 0.4.
Authority or jurisdiction is ambiguous because the manual is labeled EU and includes CE/European Authorized Representative content, but also lists FDA guidance, 510(k), 21 CFR, and FCC information.
Some OCR/table text appears imperfect, including duplicated table headings, misspellings, and inconsistent error-code descriptions.
